codeReview.summary: Pass. Relative to both prior failed reviews, the final state no longer narrows collection with `testpaths`, no longer blanket-ignores top-level `tests/test*.py` modules, restores standalone runtime-settings coverage, and explicitly collects the repaired hermetic root backend suites. Static inspection of the remaining hidden root-level `test*.py` files did not reveal any pytest-style modules without direct-run/manual markers, so the selective reclassification appears consistent with the feature goal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Repo test docs still describe `python -m pytest tests/` and root-level `test_*.py` files as if they all participate in the unattended manifest run, but `tests/conftest.py` now intentionally reclassifies most root-level `test*.py` files as direct-run/manual checks. That collection boundary is clearer in code than in shared state docs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `tests/README.md:14,56,59` and `tests/FILE_ORGANIZATION_SUMMARY.md:9,98` still present the root `test_*.py` files as part of the general pytest story, while `tests/conftest.py:31-33,68-71` now documents and enforces selective root-level collection.

summary: Pass: this rerun adequately addresses both prior blocking scrutiny failures. The repository state now removes the earlier `testpaths` narrowing, replaces the old blanket top-level ignore with explicit collection of repaired backend root suites, and re-enables runtime-settings coverage without leaving any obvious hidden automated root pytest modules behind.
